questions.question: What is the benefit of creating a test case directly from a requirement?
options: It automatically generates test steps from the requirement text
It automatically populates the Verifies field with the traceability link
It skips the approval workflow for the test case
It creates a test run at the same time as the test case
questions.rationale: Creating a test case as a downstream reference from a requirement automatically populates the Verifies field, establishing the traceability link without manual configuration. It does not generate steps, skip approval, or create test runs automatically.

questions.question: What happens when a test step uses the ${paramName} syntax?
options: The step is skipped during execution
Codebeamer creates separate test case instances for each parameter value during execution
The parameter value is permanently hardcoded into the step
The test case is automatically marked as critical
questions.rationale: Parameters with ${paramName} syntax reference values from the test case's parameter table. During a test run, Codebeamer creates separate test case instances for each parameter row, allowing multiple input/output combinations to be tested from a single test case definition.

questions.question: What does a suspected badge indicate in Codebeamer?
options: The item has a bug reported against it
The item's upstream reference has changed and it may need review
The item has not been approved by the test lead
The item is scheduled for deletion
questions.rationale: A suspected badge appears when an upstream reference changes, indicating the downstream item may be outdated and needs review. For example, if a requirement changes after a test case was written for it, the test case becomes suspect because it may no longer accurately validate the updated requirement.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a method for creating test cases in Codebeamer?
options: Create from the test case tracker page
Create as a downstream reference from a requirement
Import from an Excel file
Automatically generate from test run results
questions.rationale: Test cases can be created from the tracker page, from requirements (downstream reference), by importing from Excel, or by reusing templates from a library. Test cases cannot be automatically generated from test run results — test runs execute existing test cases, they don't create new ones.

questions.question: What is the role of the Critical flag on a test step?
options: It highlights the step in red for visual emphasis
It requires two testers to verify the step
It stops the entire test if that step fails
It sends an email notification when the step is executed
questions.rationale: The Critical flag on a test step means that if the step fails during execution, the entire test stops at that step. This prevents further steps from executing when a critical validation has already failed, ensuring that critical failures are immediately addressed.
